mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate hikes are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals weakening consumer demand and rising recession probability, which forces equity markets to price in lower future earnings and produces negative forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above 0.5pp monthly increase threshold
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of instances where unemployment rises above the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business confidence, driving immediate equity inflows and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative forward returns in greater than 50% of cases or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening economic momentum that immediately boosts corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during inflationary supply shocks or Rank IC falls below 0.01 over 1-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained inflation above the 3% annual threshold triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL crosses above 3.0% annual inflation threshold
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite high inflation readings, or the signal fails to predict returns when inflation is supply-driven rather than demand-driven.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment indicates robust labor market absorption and wage growth, supporting consumer spending and generating positive equity returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The payroll-unemployment divergence coincides with negative equity returns in greater than 55% of observations or loses predictive power during structural labor shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth above trend reflects expanding corporate profit pools and economic resilience, which systematically drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP revisions consistently fail to align with equity performance or the signal produces negative returns in greater than 50% of positive growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly contraction in industrial production signals immediate supply chain disruptions or demand collapse, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO crosses below -1.0% monthly growth threshold
failure_condition: Markets ignore industrial production drops and post positive returns in greater than 60% of triggered instances, or the threshold fails to capture structural manufacturing declines.
